summary: This brief meta-post announces several recent publications by the author across different platforms. O'Neil explains her reduced blogging frequency is due to writing commitments elsewhere, then provides links to four pieces: an article arguing Facebook's algorithm threatens democracy, a co-authored letter critiquing FBI machine learning proposals, responses to her book from Data & Society, and a Quartz interview. The post functions primarily as a directory to her work published in more prominent venues.
key_points: O'Neil argues Facebook's algorithm is 'winning' against democracy in her Nova piece
She collaborated with Susan Landau to push back against proposals for FBI use of machine learning
Her book 'Weapons of Math Destruction' generated substantive responses from Data & Society researchers
The post reflects her transition from independent blogging to writing for established media outlets
money_quotes: If you're wondering why I don't write more blog posts, it's because I'm writing for other stuff all the time now!
key_insight: As algorithmic accountability becomes mainstream, critics like O'Neil are shifting from blogs to major media platforms where their arguments about Facebook and FBI surveillance reach broader audiences.
